pattern: Count non-decreasing partitions of a digit string with a two-index DP, using longest-common-prefix comparisons and prefix sums to fold the equal-length case in constant time.
hints: Track partitions by where the final number begins; a new number appended must be at least as large as the one before it, and no number may start with a zero.
Two numbers of different lengths compare by length alone; equal-length numbers compare lexicographically, which a precomputed longest-common-prefix table answers in O(1).

Let ways[i][j] be the count of valid partitions of the prefix ending at i whose last piece starts at j. A prefix-sum over j lets you add all shorter previous pieces at once, and the LCP table decides the single equal-length boundary case.

edge_inputs.description: single zero is not a positive integer
single nonzero digit, only the whole string
all equal digits allow many non-decreasing splits
internal zeros forbid splits that would start a piece with zero

stress.description: random short digit strings (no forced leading-zero handling)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 120))
    s = ''.join(str(rng.randrange(0, 10)) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
stress.note: kept small so the O(n^3) oracle stays fast
